childhood.familyBackground: Jewish heritage, grandson of Marvin Lewis, a California trial attorney and BART champion. Family valued education and intellectual pursuits.
childhood.familyBackgroundKo: 유대인 혈통이며, 캘리포니아 변호사이자 BART 챔피언인 마빈 루이스의 손자다. 가족은 교육과 지적 추구를 중시했다.
childhood.education: Burlingame High School (1982), Bachelor of Science in Business Administration from University of Southern California (1986), member of Tau Kappa Epsilon fraternity
childhood.educationKo: 벌링게임 고등학교 (1982), 남캘리포니아 대학교 경영학 학사 (1986), 타우 카파 엡실론 형제회 회원
childhood.earlyLife: Showed entrepreneurial spirit from youth. At age 14, sold software 'How to Juggle' for $75. Founded Liberty Software at age 15, creating Atari video games like 'Flapper' and 'King Arthur's Heir'. Royalties financed college education. Worked as assembly language programmer at Apple's Macintosh Division, inspired by Steve Jobs.
childhood.earlyLifeKo: 어린 시절부터 기업가 정신을 보였다. 14세에 'How to Juggle' 소프트웨어를 75달러에 판매했다. 15세에 Liberty Software를 설립하여 'Flapper'와 'King Arthur's Heir' 같은 아타리 비디오 게임을 만들었다. 로열티로 대학 교육비를 충당했다. 스티브 잡스에 영감을 받아 Apple의 Macintosh 부서에서 어셈블리어 프로그래머로 일했다.

careerTimeline.event: Joined Apple Computer's Macintosh Division as assembly language programmer, inspired by Steve Jobs
careerTimeline.eventKo: 스티브 잡스에 영감을 받아 Apple Computer의 Macintosh 부서에 어셈블리어 프로그래머로 입사했다

careerTimeline.event: Graduated from USC with degree in Business Administration; joined Oracle Corporation
careerTimeline.eventKo: USC에서 경영학 학위로 졸업했으며 Oracle Corporation에 입사했다

careerTimeline.event: Named Oracle's Rookie of the Year at age 23
careerTimeline.eventKo: 23세에 Oracle의 Rookie of the Year로 선정되었다

careerTimeline.event: Promoted to Vice President at Oracle, youngest person to hold that title at the company
careerTimeline.eventKo: Oracle의 부사장으로 승진하여 회사 최연소 부사장이 되었다

careerTimeline.event: Took six-month sabbatical to India and Hawaii, developed vision for cloud-based CRM during reflection period
careerTimeline.eventKo: 인도와 하와이로 6개월 안식년을 다녀왔으며, 성찰 기간 동안 클라우드 기반 CRM에 대한 비전을 개발했다

careerTimeline.event: Left Oracle at age 35 with $2 million investment from Larry Ellison; co-founded Salesforce with Parker Harris, Dave Moellenhoff, and Frank Dominguez
careerTimeline.eventKo: 35세에 래리 엘리슨의 200만 달러 투자로 Oracle을 떠났으며, 파커 해리스, 데이브 뫼렌호프, 프랭크 도미네즈와 함께 Salesforce를 공동 창립했다

careerTimeline.event: Salesforce launched first product from one-bedroom apartment atop Telegraph Hill in San Francisco
careerTimeline.eventKo: Salesforce가 샌프란시스코 Telegraph Hill 꼭대기의 원룸 아파트에서 첫 제품을 출시했다

careerTimeline.event: Executed aggressive cost-cutting measures, laid off 20% of workforce (20 people from 100) with co-founder Parker Harris to save the company
careerTimeline.eventKo: 공동 창업자 파커 해리스와 함께 회사를 구하기 위해 공격적인 비용 절감을 단행하여 인력의 20% (100명 중 20명)을 해고했다

careerTimeline.event: Organized fake protest at Siebel annual conference claiming 'Software is Obsolete,' gained significant media coverage and new customers
careerTimeline.eventKo: Siebel 연례 컨퍼런스에서 '소프트웨어는 구식이다'고 주장하는 가짜 시위를 조직하여 상당한 언론 보도와 신규 고객을 확보했다

careerTimeline.event: Salesforce went public in June 2004, raising $110 million; became first major SaaS company to achieve massive scale
careerTimeline.eventKo: Salesforce가 2004년 6월 상장하여 1억 1천만 달러를 조달했으며, 대규모 성장을 달성한 첫 번째 주요 SaaS 회사가 되었다

careerTimeline.event: Published 'Behind the Cloud: The Untold Story of How Salesforce.com Went from Idea to Billion-Dollar Company'
careerTimeline.eventKo: 'Behind the Cloud: The Untold Story of How Salesforce.com Went from Idea to Billion-Dollar Company'를 출판했다

careerTimeline.event: Net worth reached approximately $3.8 billion according to Forbes
careerTimeline.eventKo: 포브스에 따르면 순자산이 약 38억 달러에 도달했다

careerTimeline.event: Emerged as leading advocate for stakeholder capitalism at Davos and globally
careerTimeline.eventKo: 다보스와 세계적으로 이해관계자 자본주의의 주요 옹호자로 부상했다

careerTimeline.event: Became co-CEO with Bret Taylor; published 'Trailblazer: The Power of Business as the Greatest Platform for Change'
careerTimeline.eventKo: Bret Taylor와 함께 공동 CEO가 되었으며 'Trailblazer: The Power of Business as the Greatest Platform for Change'를 출판했다

careerTimeline.event: Bret Taylor stepped down as co-CEO; Benioff continued as sole CEO
careerTimeline.eventKo: Bret Taylor가 공동 CEO에서 물러났고 Benioff가 단독 CEO로 계속했다

failures.description: Salesforce faced critical cash crisis in early stages with lack of CFO and limited capital; forced to lay off 20% of workforce (20 employees from 100-person team) with co-founder Parker Harris visibly upset
failures.descriptionKo: Salesforce는 초기 단계에서 CFO 부재와 제한된 자본으로 인한 심각한 현금 위기에 직면했으며, 공동 창업자 파커 해리스가 명백히 불쾌해하면서 인력의 20% (100명 팀 중 20명)을 해고해야 했다
failures.lesson: Aggressive cost-cutting and difficult decisions can be necessary for survival; emotional accountability matters in leadership
failures.lessonKo: 공격적인 비용 절감과 어려운 결정이 생존을 위해 필요할 수 있으며, 감정적 책임감이 리더십에 중요하다

failures.description: Salesforce nearly lost its largest client Merrill Lynch when the COO told thousands of advisers that the company ought to drop Salesforce, with the entire room applauding. Feedback revealed 'No one likes your product here'
failures.descriptionKo: Salesforce의 최대 클라이언트인 Merrill Lynch를 거의 잃을 뻔했을 때 COO가 수천 명의 고문에게 회사가 Salesforce를 떨어뜨려야 한다고 말했으며, 전체 방이 박수를 쳤다. 피드백은 '여기서 아무도 당신의 제품을 좋아하지 않는다'는 것을 드러냈다
failures.lesson: Customer success requires deeper understanding of user pain points and organizational receptivity; need to reassess service delivery and customer relationships
failures.lessonKo: 고객 성공을 위해서는 사용자 고충과 조직적 수용성을 더 깊이 이해해야 하며, 서비스 제공과 고객 관계를 재평가할 필요가 있다
failures.source: Built In, Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff

failures.description: Announced mass dismissal of approximately 7,000 employees (approximately 8% of workforce) via two-hour all-hands meeting over a call. Employees criticized Benioff for being evasive during meeting and forcing 'Ohana' culture messaging during job loss announcement
failures.descriptionKo: 2시간 전사 회의를 통해 약 7,000명의 직원(인력의 약 8%)을 대량 해고한다고 발표했다. 직원들은 Benioff가 회의 중 회피적이었고 일자리 손실 발표 중에 'Ohana' 문화 메시지를 강제했다고 비판했다
failures.lesson: Leadership communication must be sensitive to context; transparency and empathy are essential during difficult organizational changes; poor execution of necessary decisions damages trust
failures.lessonKo: 리더십 커뮤니케이션은 상황에 민감해야 하며, 어려운 조직 변화 중에 투명성과 공감이 필수적이다; 필요한 결정의 형편없는 실행은 신뢰를 손상시킨다

quotes.text: Capitalism as we have known it is dead. This obsession that we have with maximizing profits for shareholders alone has led to incredible inequality and a planetary emergency.
quotes.textKo: 우리가 알던 자본주의는 죽었다. 주주만을 위해 이윤을 극대화하려는 집착이 엄청난 불평등과 행성 비상사태를 초래했다.
quotes.context: Statement on the need for stakeholder capitalism, emphasizing broader corporate responsibility beyond shareholder returns
quotes.contextKo: 이해관계자 자본주의의 필요성에 대한 성명으로, 주주 수익 이상의 광범위한 기업 책임을 강조한다

quotes.text: The old capitalism was all about the business of business is business. The new stakeholder capitalism is about business as the greatest platform for change.
quotes.textKo: 이전의 자본주의는 사업은 사업이다는 것이 전부였다. 새로운 이해관계자 자본주의는 변화를 위한 가장 강력한 플랫폼으로서의 사업을 다룬다.
quotes.context: Defining the shift in business philosophy toward recognizing multiple stakeholders
quotes.contextKo: 여러 이해관계자를 인식하는 방향으로의 사업 철학의 전환을 정의한다

quotes.text: The great miscalculation of the age is the idea that businesses have to make a choice: to become profitable, or to become platforms for change. This is not the case.
quotes.textKo: 이 시대의 큰 오산은 사업이 이윤을 추구하거나 변화의 플랫폼이 되어야 한다는 선택을 해야 한다는 생각이다. 그렇지 않다.
quotes.context: From his book Trailblazer, arguing that profit and purpose are not mutually exclusive
quotes.contextKo: 그의 저서 Trailblazer에서 이윤과 목적이 상호 배타적이지 않다고 주장한다
quotes.source: Trailblazer: The Power of Business as the Greatest Platform for Change

quotes.text: We need a new capitalism that is more fair, more equitable, more sustainable. Capitalism that values not just all shareholders, but all stakeholders.
quotes.textKo: 더 공정하고, 더 공평하고, 더 지속 가능한 새로운 자본주의가 필요하다. 모든 주주뿐만 아니라 모든 이해관계자를 가치 있게 여기는 자본주의.
quotes.context: Vision for business model transformation including employees, customers, communities, and planet
quotes.contextKo: 직원, 고객, 지역사회, 행성을 포함한 사업 모델 변환에 대한 비전

quotes.text: Those that get stuck and don't have a beginner's mind, they get in trouble because there's too many things happening too fast.
quotes.textKo: 갇혀있고 초심자 정신이 없는 사람들은 너무 많은 일이 너무 빠르게 일어나기 때문에 곤경에 빠진다.
quotes.context: On adopting 'shoshin' (beginner's mind), his core leadership philosophy
quotes.contextKo: 초심(初心) (초심자 정신)을 채택하는 것에 관해, 그의 핵심 리더십 철학

authored.title: Behind the Cloud: The Untold Story of How Salesforce.com Went from Idea to Billion-Dollar Company-and Revolutionized an Industry
authored.note: Co-authored with Carlye Adler (2009). Shares strategies that inspired employees, turned customers into evangelists, and enabled innovation to flourish at Salesforce
authored.noteKo: Carlye Adler와 공동 저술 (2009). 직원들에게 영감을 주고 고객을 전도사로 변모시키고 Salesforce에서 혁신이 꽃피울 수 있게 한 전략을 공유한다

authored.title: Trailblazer: The Power of Business as the Greatest Platform for Change
authored.note: Published 2021 with Monica Langley. Reveals how Salesforce's core values—trust, customer success, innovation, and equality—became competitive advantages and engines of success
authored.noteKo: Monica Langley와 2021년 발행. Salesforce의 핵심 가치인 신뢰, 고객 성공, 혁신, 평등이 어떻게 경쟁 우위와 성공의 원동력이 되었는지 보여준다

authored.title: The Business of Changing the World
authored.note: Featuring 20 great leaders revealing how businesses can leverage full scope of resources to make a difference beyond charitable donations
authored.noteKo: 20명의 위대한 리더들을 소개하며 사업이 자선 기부를 초월하여 차이를 만들기 위해 전체 자원을 어떻게 활용할 수 있는지를 보여준다

authored.title: Compassionate Capitalism
authored.note: First-ever best-practices guide for corporate philanthropy illustrating integrated model of social responsibility
authored.noteKo: 기업 자선 활동의 최초 모범 사례 가이드로 사회적 책임의 통합 모델을 보여준다

recommended.title: The Innovator's Dilemma
recommended.author: Clayton Christensen
recommended.why: Benioff contributed foreword to updated edition; influential on understanding disruptive innovation in technology and business models
recommended.whyKo: Benioff는 업데이트된 판의 서문을 기여했으며, 기술과 사업 모델의 파괴적 혁신을 이해하는 데 영향력이 있다

recommended.title: Steve Jobs: The Exclusive Biography
recommended.author: Walter Isaacson
recommended.why: Understanding the visionary approach of Jobs, who mentored Benioff at Apple and shaped his philosophy of design and simplicity
recommended.whyKo: Apple에서 Benioff를 멘토링했으며 그의 디자인과 단순성의 철학을 형성한 잡스의 선견적 접근 방식을 이해한다

recommended.title: Good to Great: Why Some Companies Make the Leap and Others Don't
recommended.author: Jim Collins
recommended.why: Framework for building sustained excellence that aligns with Benioff's values-driven approach to organizational culture
recommended.whyKo: 지속적 우수성을 구축하기 위한 프레임워크로 Benioff의 조직 문화에 대한 가치 중심의 접근 방식과 일치한다

personalTraits.knownFor: Visionary leadership combining technology innovation with social responsibility. Pioneer of cloud computing (SaaS) and fierce advocate for stakeholder capitalism and business ethics. Known for Buddhist philosophy integration into leadership, promoting meditation and mindfulness in corporate culture. Strong communicator and storyteller. Accessible, people-focused CEO who prioritizes employee equality and customer success.
personalTraits.knownForKo: 기술 혁신과 사회적 책임을 결합한 선견적 리더십. 클라우드 컴퓨팅 (SaaS)의 개척자이자 이해관계자 자본주의와 사업 윤리의 강력한 옹호자. 리더십에 불교 철학을 통합하고 기업 문화에서 명상과 마음챙김을 촉진하는 것으로 알려져 있다. 강한 소통 능력과 이야기 능력. 직원 평등과 고객 성공을 우선시하는 접근 가능하고 사람 중심의 CEO.
personalTraits.philanthropy: Major philanthropist through Salesforce Foundation. Committed to public education, nonprofits, and LGBTQ causes. Signed The Giving Pledge. Integrated 1-1-1 model at Salesforce: 1% of equity, 1% of product, 1% of employees' time to philanthropy. Strong supporter of climate action and community impact initiatives.
personalTraits.philanthropyKo: Salesforce 재단을 통한 주요 자선가. 공교육, 비영리 기관, LGBTQ 원인에 헌신하고 있다. The Giving Pledge에 서명했다. Salesforce에 1-1-1 모델을 통합: 지분의 1%, 제품의 1%, 직원 시간의 1%를 자선에 기부한다. 기후 행동과 지역사회 영향 이니셔티브의 강력한 지지자.
personalTraits.controversies: 2023 mass layoffs executed insensitively through all-hands call announcement, damaging employee trust despite layoffs being financially necessary. Activist investors have criticized expensive acquisition habits. Historical tensions with Oracle's Larry Ellison (mentor turned rival) over cloud strategy. Criticism for promoting 'Ohana' (family) culture while conducting major layoffs seen as contradictory. Some skepticism about sincere commitment to stakeholder capitalism versus public relations
personalTraits.controversiesKo: 2023년 전사 회의 공지를 통한 무분별한 대량 해고로 직원 신뢰를 손상시켰으며, 해고가 재정적으로 필요함에도 불구하고 그렇다. 행동주의 투자자들은 비싼 인수 습관을 비판했다. 클라우드 전략을 둘러싼 Oracle의 Larry Ellison (멘토에서 라이벌로)과의 역사적 긴장. 주요 해고를 하면서 'Ohana' (가족) 문화를 촉진하는 것으로 비판받으며 모순으로 보인다. 이해관계자 자본주의에 대한 진정한 헌신 대 홍보에 대한 회의론

pivotalMentors.name: Steve Jobs
pivotalMentors.relationship: Supervisor at Apple Macintosh Division; early career mentor
pivotalMentors.influence: Shaped Benioff's philosophy on design simplicity, obsessive customer focus, and mixing creativity with technology. Jobs' visionary leadership approach directly influenced Salesforce's innovation culture and values-driven business model

pivotalMentors.name: Larry Ellison
pivotalMentors.relationship: CEO and mentor at Oracle Corporation (13-year employment); ongoing relationship and early investor
pivotalMentors.influence: Taught Benioff sales excellence, executive presence, and business strategy. Ellison's $2 million initial investment in Salesforce was critical. Benioff studied Ellison's leadership playbook and executive development approach, though ultimately diverged on philosophy around stakeholder capitalism

pivotalMentors.name: Warren Bennis
pivotalMentors.relationship: Mentor during bachelor's degree and early career
pivotalMentors.influence: Shaped Benioff's views on corporate culture, transformational leadership principles, and organizational development that later defined Salesforce's culture

additionalInsights.dailyRoutine: Wakes around 5:00 AM to practice mindfulness meditation and breathwork for mental clarity. Allocates early morning hours to strategic conversations with management team assessing key success metrics. Lunch often becomes networking occasion with key partners and customers. Late afternoons dedicated to self-growth through reading leadership material, technology insights, and attending professional gatherings. Evening stroll provides reflection time. Dinner with family when possible for mental decompression. Consistent evening routine aids decision-making clarity.
keyAnecdotes: As young entrepreneur, went door-to-door trying to sell antenna and CB radio repairs, and worked cleaning jewelry store before discovering computer at Radio Shack across street where he wrote first software
During sabbatical to India and Hawaii in mid-1990s while at Oracle, experienced spiritual awakening that committed him to building a company reflecting personal values, not just financial goals
Founded Salesforce from one-bedroom apartment atop Telegraph Hill in San Francisco with closet serving as server room, embodying bootstrap entrepreneurialism

In 2000, orchestrated fake protest at Siebel annual conference with signs claiming 'Software is Obsolete' and 'The Internet is Really Neat'—protest was so convincing it gained real news coverage and brought hundreds of new customers
Experienced transparency crisis when Salesforce had technical outage; response was making internal monitoring system public, realizing that complete transparency was key to restoring customer trust
additionalInsights.turningPoint: The six-month sabbatical to India and Hawaii in 1995 while working at Oracle was the pivotal transformation moment. Having achieved success at Oracle—rising to Vice President at 26, making over $1 million annually—Benioff felt existentially empty. This reflection period led him to envision a different type of company that would combine profitability with purpose, technology with human values, and business success with social responsibility. This philosophy directly led to founding Salesforce with integrated values of trust, customer success, innovation, and equality at its core, and later to his global advocacy for stakeholder capitalism.
additionalInsights.managementStyle: Values-driven transformational leadership emphasizing culture as competitive advantage. Practices beginner's mind (shoshin) philosophy—staying open to learning and adaptable to rapid change. Implements 'Ohana' (family) culture prioritizing employee well-being, equal pay for equal work, and unlimited paid time off. Customer obsession through V2MOM framework (vision, values, methods, obstacles, metrics) for organizational alignment. Emphasizes meditation and mindfulness as organizational practices. Collaborative and accessible approach encouraging team voice and creative ideation. People-first philosophy viewing employees as biggest company asset. Integrates Buddhist concepts into secular business leadership. Balances aggressive innovation with social responsibility. Data-driven decision making combined with empathetic communication. Transparency as trust-building mechanism despite initial discomfort.
